perturbation.original_text: Sponsor / Organization Name (“ Sponsor ”) Sponsor / Organisation Address
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor / Organization Name (“ Funder ”) Sponsor / Organisation Address
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Sponsor' to 'Funder' creates confusion, as the rest of the document consistently uses 'Sponsor'. This introduces ambiguity regarding which entity holds the rights and responsibilities initially assigned to the 'Sponsor'. This change contradicts section 3.2, which explicitly defines ownership of project IP by the Sponsor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 PROJECT IP : The Sponsor will own all right, title and interes t in and to the Project IP other than copyright in Publications as that term is defined i n the following section. The Sponsor grants each of McGill and the Intern, and if applicable the Aca demic Supervisor and members of the research team shall forever retain a royalty-free, non-exclu sive, perpetual, irrevocable license to use the Project IP for research, scholarly publication, edu cational and other non-commercial purposes.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Sponsor' to 'Funder' in the definition section, while section 3.2 explicitly grants ownership of project IP to the 'Sponsor', creates a direct contradiction. This misalignment of terminology introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the assignment of ownership rights in section 3.2. The change impairs the clarity and enforceability of the contract by making it unclear which entity, 'Sponsor' or 'Funder', owns the project IP.

perturbation.original_text: 2. DEFINITIONS a. Intern : the graduate student or post-doctoral fellow (“ PDF ”) participating in Mitacs Accelerate or Mitacs Elevate b. Academic Supervisor : the academic supervisor of an Intern.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. DEFINITIONS a. Trainee : the graduate student or post-doctoral fellow (“ PDF ”) participating in Mitacs Accelerate or Mitacs Elevate b. Academic Supervisor : the academic supervisor of an Trainee.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Intern' to 'Trainee' only in the definitions section creates an in-text contradiction because the term 'Intern' is still used throughout the document, making it unclear whether all instances of 'Intern' should now be interpreted as 'Trainee'. This affects many sections, especially those outlining the Intern's responsibilities and rights, particularly those responsibilities mentioned in section 20.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 20. RESPONSIBILITIES OF PARTIES : No Party or Project participant will be responsib le to the others for non-performance or delay in performance occasio ned by any cause beyond its control, including, without limitation, acts or omissions of the other party, acts of civil or military authority, strikes , lockouts, embargoes, insurrections or Acts of God. If any suc h delay occurs, any applicable time period shall be automatically extended for a period equal to the ti me lost provided that the party affected gives the other party prompt notice of such delay and makes r easonable efforts to correct the reason for the del ay. For greater certainty, the unavailability of the In tern or the Intern’s failure to fulfill his or her obligations pursuant to the Project are matters beyond the cont rol of McGill. In the event this occurs, the Sponso r may elect to terminate the Project but shall have n o recourse or remedy against McGill or the Academic Supervisor.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Intern' to 'Trainee' in the definition section, while the term 'Intern' is still used in section 20, creates a contradiction. Section 20 refers to the 'unavailability of the Intern or the Intern’s failure to fulfill his or her obligations'. Because the term 'Intern' was changed to 'Trainee' in the Definitions section while still being used later in the document, it's unclear if the responsibilities mentioned in section 20 now apply to 'Trainees' or if 'Intern' refers to a different role altogether. This contradiction affects the enforceability of the responsibilities and obligations outlined in section 20, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.  OWNERSHIP AND USE OF INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY : 3.1 BACKGROUND IP : Each Party will retain all right, title and inter est in and to its Background IP, provided or used in the Project and no license to u se any Background IP is granted or implied by this Agreement excepting that each Party will be de emed to have been granted a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to use the Background IP of a nother Party which that Party makes available for use in a Project, but only to the extent reason ably necessary to undertake that Project.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  OWNERSHIP AND USE OF INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY : 3.1 PRIOR ART : Each Party will retain all right, title and inter est in and to its Background IP, provided or used in the Project and no license to u se any Background IP is granted or implied by this Agreement excepting that each Party will be de emed to have been granted a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to use the Background IP of a nother Party which that Party makes available for use in a Project, but only to the extent reason ably necessary to undertake that Project.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the term 'Background IP' with 'Prior Art' only in section 3.1 creates a contradiction. The document continues to use 'Background IP' in subsequent sections, particularly 3.2, which discusses licensing rights based on 'Background IP'. If these terms are not explicitly defined as synonymous (and they often are not in legal contexts), it raises uncertainty about whether section 3.2 applies to what is defined as 'Prior Art' in 3.1, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 PROJECT IP : The Sponsor will own all right, title and interes t in and to the Project IP other than copyright in Publications as that term is defined i n the following section. The Sponsor grants each of McGill and the Intern, and if applicable the Aca demic Supervisor and members of the research team shall forever retain a royalty-free, non-exclu sive, perpetual, irrevocable license to use the Project IP for research, scholarly publication, edu cational and other non-commercial purposes.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Background IP' with 'Prior Art' in section 3.1. The explanation states this creates a contradiction because the document continues to use 'Background IP' in subsequent sections, such as 3.2. Section 3.2 discusses licensing rights based on 'Background IP'. Because the terms are not defined as synonymous, it creates uncertainty about whether section 3.2 applies to what is defined as 'Prior Art' in 3.1, creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.4 PUBLICATION : The Parties are not restricted from presenting, p ublishing or otherwise disseminating the results of any research relating to a Project (including without limitation the Project IP) at symposia, professional meetings or f or academic evaluation or other academic purposes (including, without limitation, publishing thesis, course reports, journal articles or other academic publications) (a “Publication”) provided t hat during the Project and for a period of two years following the completion or other termination of the Project, the Academic Supervisor and the Intern will provide the Sponsor with a copy of any proposed Publication containing the results of any research relating to a Project or any Projec t IP at least thirty (30) days in advance of the proposed publication date.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4 DISSEMINATION : The Parties are not restricted from presenting, p ublishing or otherwise disseminating the results of any research relating to a Project (including without limitation the Project IP) at symposia, professional meetings or f or academic evaluation or other academic purposes (including, without limitation, publishing thesis, course reports, journal articles or other academic publications) (a “Publication”) provided t hat during the Project and for a period of two years following the completion or other termination of the Project, the Academic Supervisor and the Intern will provide the Sponsor with a copy of any proposed DISSEMINATION containing the results of any research relating to a Project or any Projec t IP at least thirty (30) days in advance of the proposed publication date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Publication' to 'Dissemination' in the second instance within section 3.4 creates confusion. The term 'Publication' remains defined and is used elsewhere in the clause, creating uncertainty about whether the advance notice requirement applies to all disseminations or only those classified as 'Publications', causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4 PUBLICATION : The Parties are not restricted from presenting, p ublishing or otherwise disseminating the results of any research relating to a Project (including without limitation the Project IP) at symposia, professional meetings or f or academic evaluation or other academic purposes (including, without limitation, publishing thesis, course reports, journal articles or other academic publications) (a “Publication”) provided t hat during the Project and for a period of two years following the completion or other termination of the Project, the Academic Supervisor and the Intern will provide the Sponsor with a copy of any proposed DISSEMINATION containing the results of any research relating to a Project or any Projec t IP at least thirty (30) days in advance of the proposed publication date. The Sponsor may, within fifteen (15) days following receipt of a copy of the proposed publication require by written noti ce to McGill and the Intern that the proposed publication date be delayed, for a period of not gr eater than sixty (60) days, so as to permit the Sponsor to make an application to register a patent with respect to the Project IP. Failing receipt of such notice in the time and in the manner provid ed, McGill and the Intern will be at liberty to publish the proposed publication without further no tice to the Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Publication' with 'Dissemination' in a subsequent reference within section 3.4, while maintaining the original definition of 'Publication' and referencing it elsewhere in the same section (as shown in the contradicted text). This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the advance notice requirement applies to all disseminations or only those classified as 'Publications.' This uncertainty and potential conflict in obligations, aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', as 'Publication' and 'Dissemination' are used without clear distinction, leading to conflicting obligations regarding the advance notice requirement.

perturbation.original_text: 5. DESIGNATED REPRESENTATIVES : Each party designates a representative for coordi nating receipt, release and delivery of Confidential Information, w hich for McGill will be Intern AND Name of Academic Supervisor and/or Office of Sponsored Research repr esentative and for Sponsor: Name of designated representative for Sponsor, or another individual(s ) as the Party may designate in writing to the othe r Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. DESIGNATED CONTACTS : Each party designates a contact for coordi nating receipt, release and delivery of Confidential Information, w hich for McGill will be Intern AND Name of Academic Supervisor and/or Office of Sponsored Research repr esentative and for Funder: Name of designated representative for Sponsor, or another individual(s ) as the Party may designate in writing to the othe r Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Contacts' and Sponsor to 'Funder' in this section. The document elsewhere continues to use 'representative' and 'Sponsor'. It is unclear, for instance, if the notice provision in section 21 is to be sent to the "designated contacts" or the "representatives", creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 21. NOTICES : Any demand, notice or other communication to be given in connection with this Agreement shall be given in writing and shall be gi ven by personal delivery, by registered mail or by electronic means of communication addressed to Reci pient as follows: McGill : McGill Office of Sponsored Research Associate Director, Contracts and Agreements James Administration Building, 2nd Floor 845 Sherbrooke Street West Montreal, Quebec, H3A 0G4 Canada Tel: 514.398.4373 Fax: 514.398.4853 Sponsor : Sponsor Name c/o Sponsor Representative Sponsor Address Tel: Fax: Intern: Intern Name Intern McGill Address Tel: Fax:
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Contacts' and 'Sponsor' to 'Funder' introduces misaligned terminology. Section 21 refers to 'Sponsor Representative', and the changed text now uses 'Contacts' and 'Funder'. It's unclear whether notices should be sent to the 'designated contacts' as defined in the changed text or the 'Sponsor Representative' as stated in Section 21, creating a direct contradiction and uncertainty regarding who should receive official notices.
